instructions: Possible supernova detected in NGC 4526 (Virgo Cluster). Capture a confirmation image immediately using CCD with minimum 10-second exposure through luminance filter. Verify star field matches expected coordinates. NGC 4526 is a lenticular galaxy at RA 12h 34m 03s, Dec +07d 41m 57s — a known host of Type Ia supernovae (SN 1994D was discovered here).

reference.note: NGC 4526 is a real galaxy in the Virgo Cluster at distance ~55 Mly. It hosted the well-studied Type Ia supernova SN 1994D. These coordinates are from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED).
